description: Build the core application structure: index.html with tab-based navigation, CSS styling, and the core astronomical calculation engine. This establishes the foundation that all other features build upon.
steps: Create index.html with a modern, responsive tab-based UI. Tabs needed: Input, Bhava/House Analysis, BPHS Assessment, KP Assessment, Marriage Timeline, 20-Year Forecast, Health Compatibility, Ashtakoota, Dashakoota, Technical Manual, PDF Report. Include CDN links for html2pdf.js.
Create css/styles.css with professional styling for the entire application - tab navigation, tables, charts, form inputs, print styles, responsive design.

Create js/astro-core.js implementing: (a) Planetary longitude calculations using Swiss Ephemeris-style algorithms for Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu. (b) Nakshatra determination (27 nakshatras with pada). (c) Rashi (zodiac sign) calculation. (d) House/Bhava cusp calculation using Placidus or KP Ayanamsa system. (e) Ayanamsa calculation (Lahiri/Chitrapaksha). (f) Julian Day conversion from date/time/timezone.

Create js/app.js as the main controller: tab switching logic, form handling for birth details input (date, time, place with lat/long for both boy and girl), orchestration of calculations when user submits data, and rendering results into each tab's content area.
acceptance_criteria: index.html opens in browser without errors and shows all 11 tabs
Tab navigation works - clicking each tab shows its content panel
Input form accepts birth details (date, time, latitude, longitude, timezone) for both boy and girl
Core astronomical calculations produce planetary longitudes, nakshatras, and rashis for a given birth date/time/location
No JavaScript console errors on page load
verification: Open index.html in a browser and confirm all tabs render and are clickable
Check browser console for JavaScript errors
Verify the input form has fields for boy and girl birth details
